records.contentEn: Gu Yuan was one of the most influential printmakers and visual artists in twentieth-century China. Through woodcut printmaking, watercolor, and art education, he documented the social transformations of modern China and became a key figure in the development of modern Chinese visual culture.
records.contentCn: 古元是20世紀中國最具影響力的版畫家與視覺藝術家之一。他通過木刻、水彩與美術教育，記錄了現代中國社會轉型中的視覺經驗，并成為中國現代視覺文化發展的關鍵人物。

records.contentEn: Born in Nazhou Village, Tangjiawan, in present-day Zhuhai, Guangdong Province, Gu Yuan spent his early years surrounded by the rural landscapes and coastal environment of southern China. These early visual memories later shaped the strong sense of locality and human warmth found in his works.
records.contentCn: 古元出生於今天廣東珠海唐家灣那洲村，在中國南方鄉村與海岸環境中成長。早年的鄉土經驗與自然記憶，後來深刻影響了他作品中的地方氣息與人文溫度。

records.contentEn: In 1938, Gu Yuan traveled to Yan’an and studied at the Lu Xun Academy of Arts. During this period, he developed woodcut printmaking as a visual language closely connected to rural life, wartime experience, and social transformation.
records.contentCn: 1938年，古元前往延安并進入魯迅藝術學院學習。在這一時期，他逐漸將木刻發展為一種與鄉村生活、戰爭經驗與社會變遷緊密相關的視覺語言。

records.contentEn: Through works depicting land reform, labor, village life, and ordinary people, Gu Yuan helped transform modern Chinese woodcut into a form of visual social memory rooted in everyday experience.
records.contentCn: 通過描繪土地改革、勞動、鄉村生活與普通人民，古元將現代中國木刻發展為一種植根於日常經驗的社會視覺記憶形式。

records.contentEn: Later, Gu Yuan became a professor and eventually President of the Central Academy of Fine Arts in Beijing, where he played an important role in shaping modern Chinese art education and printmaking practice.
records.contentCn: 後來，古元擔任中央美術學院教授及院長，在中國現代美術教育與版畫體系建設中發揮了重要作用。

records.contentEn: Today, many of Gu Yuan’s major original works remain preserved within China and are difficult to access internationally. The Gu Yuan Digital Museum seeks to create an open platform for research, preservation, and global engagement with the visual history of twentieth-century China.
records.contentCn: 今天，古元許多重要原作仍主要保存在中國境內，國際公眾較難系統接觸。本數字博物館希望建立一個開放的研究與保存平臺，重新連接20世紀中國視覺歷史與全球觀眾。
